text: The given information reflects our current knowledge. It is based on statements and information of our suppliers and third parties. This declaration does not include guarantees of quality or durability. Topicality, completeness and accuracy of the content are subject to change without notice. The safety instructions are to be respected. The user is self-responsible for the evaluation of the information and the concrete measures derived from it. Besides that, HARTING is liable only for culpable, intentional or grossly negligent acting as long as no mandatory legal liability for damages of life, body, health or Product Liability Act exists. United States Toxic Substances Control Act Section 6(h) (Restriction of certain substances) In February 2021 the United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) published in section 6 (h) the restriction of 5 substances potentially present in Electronic Equipment. Two of these substances aren’t controlled by European legislation. https://www.epa.gov/assessing-and-managing-chemicals-under-tsca/persistent-bioaccumulative-and-toxic-pbt-chemicals-under Following substances are restricted: ◼ 2,4,6-Tris(tert-butyl)phenol (2,4,6-TTBP) CAS 732-26-3 ◼ Decabromodiphenyl ether (DecaBDE) CAS 1163-19-5 ◼ Phenol, isopropylated phosphate (3:1) (PIP (3:1)) CAS 68937-41-7 ◼ 2,4,6-Tris(tert-butyl)phenol Hexachlorobutadiene (HCBD) CAS 87-68-3 ◼ Pentachlorothiophenol (PCTP) CAS 133-49-3 HARTING products don’t contain substances TTBP, DecaDBE and HCBD. The distribution of PIP (3:1) is allowed until September 2024. The distribution of PCTP is allowed until January 2022. Substance PCTP and PIP (3:1) also are not contained in HARTING products. Based on the supplier replies we don’t expect to have any of the above mentioned substances in HARTING articles. February 2025
